text: Academic Programs - Everett Public Schools Everett Public Schools offers a wide range of academics and programs designed to challenge and inspire every learner. We are guided by the core values of our district and our role is to support the mission, vision and strategic plan. We are particularly driven by the notion that all students will graduate, be prepared for college, career and life with 21st-century skills. We define those skills as critical thinking, collaboration, creativity, communication, citizenship, and growth mindset. English Language Arts Science & Engineering Spanish Language Arts Visual and Performing Arts Social Emotional Learning Everett Public Schools uses National Geographic Reach for Reading instructional materials, developed by CENGAGE Learning®, to support K-5 teaching and learning. Curriculum Alignment: The program aligns with English Language Arts Common Core State Standards (ELA CCSS) in: Speaking and Listening Integration of 21st Century Skills Collaboration for authentic engagement Deeper learning strategies aimed at preparing students for success in college, careers, and civic life Reach for Reading emphasizes high leverage best practice strategies. A robust digital platform with resources for teachers, students, and parents. C.I.A. (Collect·Interpret·Apply), developed by Read Side by Side®, is a month-long unit for 3rd-5th grade students that focuses on text structure to help access complex literature and enhance comprehension, vocabulary, and written response skills. Everett Public Schools uses SpringBoard materials by The College Board to support ELA Common Core Standards (Grades 6-12). Focus on building student proficiency in reading, writing, speaking, listening, and collaboration. Emphasizes engagement, deeper learning, and postsecondary success. Supports 21st-century skills and aligns with AP English courses and SAT expectations. LANGUAGE! Live is an additional resource for secondary literacy, aligning with ELA CCSS. Covers Reading Foundations, Reading, Writing, Speaking, Listening, and Language. Offers a blended learning model to boost student achievement and learning. Based on research, with an overview available for further details. Secondary ELA Sequence Students need mathematics to solve problems, reason logically, compute fluently, and understand their world. The Everett Public Schools Mathematics Department is committed to ensuring success for all K–12 students through engaging, high-quality instruction; equitable access to resources; effective use of tools and technology; and ongoing assessment that measures proficiency, guides instruction, and provides timely feedback for growth. Our K–12 mathematics program builds essential skills through coherent learning progressions, fostering connections within mathematical concepts and between mathematics and the real world. Maintaining the rigor, coherence, and focus of the Common Core State Standards for Mathematics at every grade level and course is key to the strength of our program. Illustrative Mathematics K-5 Common Core Standards Illustrative Math Resources for: Middle School 6-8 High School Everett Public Schools offers a district wide science and engineering program that supports curriculum and activities for all grades and schools. Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS): Washington State has adopted the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) as the state's official science standards, also called the Washington State Science and Learning Standards (WSSLS). NGSS were created by states for states. They outline key scientific and engineering practices, concepts, and core ideas. These standards ensure that all K-12 students learn what they need to succeed in college and future careers. For more details, you can visit the NGSS website to learn about how the standards are communicated. NGSS Website WA State Science and Learning Standards Everett Public School elementary schools follow the state recommended outline for elementary school. Teachers can find Social Studies GLE's and suggested Grade Level Outlines by visiting the OSPI Social Studies website below. Students in middle school have the opportunity to learn about local, national, and world history and human and physical geography. Students read and write about history and experience interactive activities that help develop Social Studies Skills: critical reasoning, inquiry-based research, and deliberation. In 6th Grade, students learn about Ancient and Medieval World History. In 7th Grade, students learn about World Geography and Washington State History. Successfully completing Washington State History in middle school fulfills a high school graduation requirement. Beginning with the 2006/07 school year, 7th grade students have the opportunity to fulfill a high school graduation requirement by successfully completing their Washington State History course. Fulfilling this requirement in 7th grade allows students more flexibility in their future high school class schedules. If a student does not successfully meet the objectives of the middle school Washington State History class, that student will still be able to fulfill the Washington State History graduation requirement by enrolling in the high school Washington State History class during their high school career. In 8th Grade, students learn about United States History, Beginnings to Reconstruction. Students in our high schools have the opportunity to learn about World, United State History, and United States Government as well as a wide variety of electives to extend their learning beyond the basic Social Studies curriculum. By taking courses

class schedules. If a student does not successfully meet the objectives of the middle school Washington State History class, that student will still be able to fulfill the Washington State History graduation requirement by enrolling in the high school Washington State History class during their high school career. In 8th Grade, students learn about United States History, Beginnings to Reconstruction. Students in our high schools have the opportunity to learn about World, United State History, and United States Government as well as a wide variety of electives to extend their learning beyond the basic Social Studies curriculum. By taking courses in World History, United States History, and United States Government, students are fulfilling their Social Studies graduation requirements. Taking at least one additional .5 credit is also required and will fulfill entrance requirements for Washington State public four-year universities and many private colleges and universities. Students begin the required series of Social Studies courses with Modern World History, typically taken in 10th grade. Students then continue to United States History in 11th grade and United States Government in 12th Grade. Each of these required courses is also available as an Advanced Placement (AP) course. For students who did not fulfill the requirement in Middle School, Washington State History is also offered. Depending upon the high school, a wide variety of electives are offered for students who are interested in extending their social studies education beyond the basic requirements, including the following AP options: AP Human Geography, AP Microeconomics, AP Macroeconomics, AP Psychology, and AP Research Social Studies. High School Students have several choices for earning college credit while taking high school courses. When pursuing college options in high school, students and their families should be aware that, depending on the program, additional expenses may be applied such as college tuition, college textbooks, and/or transportation. Advanced Placement (AP) Advanced Placement courses prepare students to take the Advanced Placement exam for that subject area. By successfully meeting standards on the AP exam, students may earn college credit. Social Studies Advanced Placement courses offered are: For more information about Advanced Placement, students should visit the AP Central Website and see their high school counselor. College in High School College in the High School courses allow students to earn college credit from local community colleges while taking courses at their own high school. Teachers of these courses must be qualified to become adjunct faculty at the local community college. Social Studies College in the High School courses offered are: For more information, students should see their high school counselor. Students who qualify and enroll in Running Start may take college courses at local community colleges instead of attending high school courses. For more information, students should contact their high school counselor. AP United States Government and Politics AP United States History United States History Washington State History United States Government Everett Public Schools’ Spanish Language Arts curriculum supports bilingual literacy and academic success for native Spanish speakers and dual-language learners. Students build strong reading, writing, and communication skills in Spanish while deepening cultural connections and preparing for future bilingual opportunities. Everett Public Schools’ World Languages curriculum helps students develop communication skills and cultural understanding to thrive in a global society. We offer Spanish, French, German and Chinese at the high school level. In the 2015-16 school year, Everett Public Schools began offering world language testing to students in grades 7-12 to provide an opportunity to earn high school credit by demonstrating fluency in reading, writing, speaking, and listening in a language other than English. Everett Public Schools recognizes the connection between academic success and physical fitness among our children. Through community collaboration and partnerships, we will serve as a catalyst for change as we move toward reducing youth obesity and increasing the number of students graduating from high school empowered with strategies for staying fit for life! OSPI - Health and PE Learning Standards Senate Bill 5395 requires all public schools to provide comprehensive sexual health education, aligned with Health Education K-12 Learning Standards and legal provisions.. To find grade level sexual health curriculum click on the tabs below. Parents or legal guardians can review the sexual health education curriculum by submitting a written request to the school district board, the school principal, or their designee. They can also opt-out their child from sexual health education by using the Parent/Guardian opt-out form. To access the form and to learn more about Sexual Health curriculum per grade level, click the button below. High School PE Competency Credits Students who cannot complete the state-required 1.5 PE credits due to academic commitments (Running Start, Sno-Isle Skills Center, AP courses, etc.) may apply for a PE Competency Credit. Must complete PE 1 before applying. Must currently participate in a coach-supervised sport. Applications must be pre-approved by the school’s

request to the school district board, the school principal, or their designee. They can also opt-out their child from sexual health education by using the Parent/Guardian opt-out form. To access the form and to learn more about Sexual Health curriculum per grade level, click the button below. High School PE Competency Credits Students who cannot complete the state-required 1.5 PE credits due to academic commitments (Running Start, Sno-Isle Skills Center, AP courses, etc.) may apply for a PE Competency Credit. Must complete PE 1 before applying. Must currently participate in a coach-supervised sport. Applications must be pre-approved by the school’s PE Competency Coordinator. Cascade HS: Jacob Huizinga Everett HS: Tara Tri Jackson HS: David Hutt Meet with your school’s PE Competency Coordinator. Complete the PE Competency Application form. Fall deadline: 2nd Friday in September Winter deadline: 1st Friday in December Spring deadline: 2nd Friday in March Submit the Competency Packet within 2 weeks of the season ending. Fall season ends: 2nd Friday in November Winter season ends: 2nd Friday in February Spring season ends: 2nd Friday in May Complete required community athletics forms, if applicable. Retroactive PE credit is not allowed (special circumstances may be considered). Contact your PE Competency Coordinator with questions. Our arts programs follow Washington State K–12 Arts Learning Standards, giving students opportunities to create, perform, and express themselves while building important life skills. Elementary School: Hands-on lessons using the Art of Education curriculum; PTSA art docent programs; integration of SEL and STEM Middle School: Art offered as an elective; students explore personal expression using the Davis curriculum Explorations in Art and Experience Art High School: Courses range from drawing, painting, ceramics, photography, and graphic design; AP Studio Art options available; new curricula from Art of Education and Davis inspire creative voice and style Showcasing Student Work: Annual Elementary, Middle, and High School Art Shows at the Community Resource Center Elementary School: General music classes that build skills and knowledge Middle and High School: Band, orchestra, and choir with many performance opportunities throughout the year Assessment: Teachers use the Washington Music Educators Association rubric to guide learning and growth. Social and emotional learning (SEL) is a process through which adults and children learn to recognize and manage emotions, demonstrate care and concern for others, develop positive relationships, make good decisions and behave ethically, respectfully, and responsibly. Academics Department 3900 Broadway, Everett, WA 98201 The Early Learning program fosters young children's cognitive, social, and emotional development through age-appropriate education. This includes ECEAP , Play & Learn , Developmental preschool, Transition to Kindergarten and other options. Advanced Learning offers accelerated and enriched academic opportunities for students in grades K–12, including Learning Enrichment, Highly Capable programs in elementary school, and advanced course options in middle and high schools. Multilingual Learner The Multilingual Learner program provides structured language acquisition instruction to students whose native language is not English by helping them develop proficiency in English while also supporting their academic growth. Career & Technical Education The Career and Technical Education (CTE) program equips students with technical, academic, and 21st-century skills for success in the workplace and further education, offering opportunities to explore in-demand career pathways. College & Career Readiness The College & Career Readiness program prepares students with the skills and knowledge needed for post-secondary success, offering guidance in college applications, career exploration, and workforce readiness. Special Education provides tailored support and resources to help students with needs succeed academically and socially. It ensures an inclusive learning environment with individualized instruction to meet student's needs. International Baccalaureate (IB) The IB Diploma Programme is a rigorous two-year pre-university program that develops globally minded students with strong critical thinking, writing, and problem-solving skills. Dual Language Program The Dual Language Spanish Immersion program (K-5) provides an environment for students to develop a higher level of bilingualism and biliteracy skills in both English and Spanish. Lighthouse Cooperative This Lighthouse Elementary Cooperative program (K-5) blends the district curriculum with parent involvement through weekly volunteering and support for enrichment activities. Port Gardner School is a parent partnership program that offers onsite classes with certificated teachers and provides resources, guidance, and support for home-based education. High School Online Options Port Gardner School is a parent partnership program that offers onsite classes with certificated teachers and provides resources, guidance, and support for home-based education. Sequoia High School is an alternative school that offers a small, supportive environment and personalized education focused on choice, equity, and relationships.

Athletics - Everett Public Schools ​The Everett Public Schools Athletics Department offers comprehensive sports programs for students in grades 7–12, emphasizing teamwork, sportsmanship, and personal growth. Middle schools compete in the Sno-King League, while high schools participate in the Western Conference (Wesco), providing opportunities in sports such as football, basketball, soccer, wrestling, track, and volleyball. The department aims to enrich students' educational experiences through athletics, fostering values that extend beyond the classroom. High School Schedules Middle School Schedules Post-Season Brackets & Tournaments Click the links below to see the schedules for each high school: Cascade High School Everett High School HM Jackson High School High School Fall sports start on August 24, 2026 High School Winter Sports start on November 16, 2026 High School Spring Sports start on March 1, 2027 *Football begins on August 19, 2026. 2026 Bowling start date TBD. **Boys/Girls Lacrosse is offered through Everett Lacrosse Club- Please click the below button for more information. Click the links below to see the schedules for each middle school: Eisenhower Middle School Evergreen Middle School Gateway Middle School Heatherwood Middle School North Middle School Middle School Fall sports start on September 8, 2026 Middle School Winter I Sports start early November 2026 Middle School Winter II Sports start on January 19, 2027 (Girls Basketball) & Mid-February (Co-ed Wrestling) Middle School Spring Sports start on April 1, 2027 Athletics Department 3721 Oakes Avenue Everett, 98201 Access information regarding athletic registration, eligibility and participation fees for students. Stay prepared, stay connected, and support athletic success every step of the way. Athletic Registration & Fees Athletic event tickets for Everett Public Schools can be purchased online or at the gate, with digital options available for most varsity games. Click the button below to learn more. Ticketing Information Access vital resources, forms, and guidelines to support athletic programs. Empower student-athletes through informed coaching and organized planning. Coaches & Staff Resources Find important information on athlete health, safety protocols, and injury prevention. Promoting well-being on and off the field for every student-athlete. Athletic Awards celebrate student-athletes across all sports with end-of-season ceremonies recognizing excellence, teamwork, and academic dedication. Everett Public Schools’ Athletic Code of Conduct sets clear expectations for sportsmanship, responsibility, and integrity for all student-athletes. Athletic Code of Conduct Everett Memorial Stadium & Lincoln Field Rentals Everett Memorial Stadium and Lincoln Field are available for athletic events, tournaments, and community activities. Rentals are approved based on district scheduling, with school athletics taking precedence.

Calendar Note: At this time, Everett Public Schools is planning for students in grades 1-12 to start the 2026-27 school year on Wednesday, September 2, 2026. Kindergarten students would begin school the following week, on Tuesday, September 8, 2026. These dates are not final. The official student calendar is part of collective bargaining, which is expected to occur over the summer. For more information, click here. Start your Future with Us - Everett Public Schools Start your Future with Us Everett Public Schools is focused on inspiring students to achieve and thrive. This work is based on our seven CORE VALUES – Respect, Learning, Equity, Collaboration, Passion, Integrity, and Diversity. Everett Public Schools is a great place to work with 18 elementary, 5 middle, and 4 high schools, and over 2,000 staff members. Serving 20,000+ students speaking 90+ languages, it offers a diverse and supportive community with consistent funding for schools and facilities. Located 25 miles north of Seattle, Everett offers a small-town feel with easy access to city amenities and abundant outdoor activities. Start Application View Job Openings Administrative Staff Create an Account: For new applicants, start by clicking on the apply button associated to the vacancy you are interested in, this will populate the frontline application page, enter your email address and a password. If you have an active Frontline account elsewhere, you will be recognized by your email address when you begin our application and given the opportunity to import relevant information. Complete the Application: The online application form takes about 30-45 minutes. Fields marked with an asterisk are required. Your application cannot be submitted until all required fields are complete. Dates must be entered in the formats shown. Submit Your Application: Click "Next Page" to progress and "Submit" when finished. You’ll receive a confirmation of successful submission. Remember, you may return to your application to make changes and additions as needed, but you must have your user-id and password. We recommend that you print a copy of your completed application after submittal for your records Complete Additional Requirements: The online application is only part of the process. Reference forms are required and will be emailed automatically to your references once the application is submitted. Only complete applications with all supporting documents including 2 completed confidential references (1 must be from a supervisor) returned via email before the closing date and time will be screened. Letter of Introduction 3 Letters of Recommendation 2 Administrative Reference Forms, one from the current year Letter of Introduction – introducing yourself including skills, knowledge, and abilities, as well as special interests. A letter of introduction is required for each position you are applying for. Transcripts - unofficial are acceptable, must be dated and show degree earned. 2 Certificated Reference Forms and 1 of those must be from a supervisor. Letter of Introduction - Introducing yourself including skills, knowledge, and abilities, as well as special interests. Official Transcripts (for paraeducators only) 2 Classified Reference Forms and 1 of those must be from a supervisor 3900 Broadway, Everett, WA 98201 Substitute Information Substitutes are an important part of our student's learning experience. Learn how to apply, view requirements, and explore opportunities to serve as a substitute in our district. Explore hands-on learning opportunities and gain real-world experience in our schools. Apply to contribute your skills while learning from educators and staff. View detailed pay scales for all staff positions across the district. Access the latest information on salaries, stipends, and career progression. Everett Public Schools provides employees with medical, dental, vision, life insurance, long-term disability, and optional benefits like retirement and flexible spending accounts. Collective Bargaining Our district works closely with employee unions to negotiate agreements on wages, benefits, and working conditions, ensuring a supportive work environment for all staff. Helpful tips, guidance, and resources to make exploring job opportunities and navigating the employment process easier, faster, and more confident for every applicant. Everett Public Schools does not discriminate in any programs or activities on the basis of sex, race, creed, religion, color, national origin, age, veteran or military status, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, disability, or the use of a trained dog guide or service animal and provides equal access to the Boy Scouts and other designated youth groups. The following employees have been designated to handle questions and complaints of alleged discrimination: Title IX/Civil Rights Compliance Officer and ADA Coordinator PO Box 2098, Everett WA 98213 SBryant@everettsd.org Section 504 Coordinator PO Box 2098, Everett WA 98213 DPeters@everettsd.org Gender-Inclusive Schools Coordinator PO Box 2098, Everett WA 98213 JGrant@everettsd.org

Enrollment - Everett Public Schools Welcome to Everett Public Schools! We are proud to serve students at all grade levels, offering a wide range of academic programs, extracurricular activities, and supportive resources to help every student thrive. Enrolling with us opens the door to a vibrant learning community where curiosity, growth, and success are encouraged every step of the way. We are excited to help you get your child enrolled with our easy Online Enrollment system. You can use any computer or mobile device to complete the process, which takes about 30 minutes. Our secure online enrollment system makes registering your child simple and convenient. Submit forms, upload documents, and track your progress - all in one place. Step 1 : Find my School and Route Step 2 : Have documents ready In Everett Public Schools, students are assigned to a school based on their primary residence address. They are expected to enroll in their designated neighborhood (attendance area) school unless they have an approved application for a School Transfer, Program Placement, or Choice Program. Not sure which school your child should attend? Use the link below to locate your neighborhood school based on your home address - it's the first step in connecting with the right school community. Find My School & Route You can upload scanned or photographed copies of your required documents during online registration. This can save you a trip to the school office. If you do not have electronic copies of your documents, you can still register online; you will just need to bring your documents to the school to complete your enrollment submission. It is helpful if you are using your phone to enroll online to take pictures of your documents with your phone before you begin. 1. Proof of Address: Acceptable documents include Current utility bill, homeowner's policy, etc. The document must show the parent/guardian's name and address. Documents should also be dated within the last 6 months. 2. Proof of Age: Required for Kindergarten or Grade 1 students who have not been previously enrolled in a US school: A religious, hospital, or physician's certificate showing date of birth Previously verified school records An affidavit from a parent An entry in a family Bible Or any other documents permitted by law. 3. Certificate of Immunization Status : Must be printed either from WA MyIR or a WAIIS Certificate of Immunization Status (CIS), or a copy signed by a physician. 4. Other Documents : May include but not limited to custody and legal documents, medication orders, prior report cards, withdrawal forms, unofficial transcripts. For enrollment-related inquiries, please reach out directly to the school. Schools Contact Information Printable Enrollment Forms For a list of frequently asked questions please scroll down this page. Step 3 : Complete the Online Registration Form Yes, we are a returning or previously enrolled family No, we are new to Everett Public Schools If you have ever had a student enrolled in Everett Public Schools, you already have an existing ParentVUE account. Please do not create a new account. If you’ve forgotten your password, click “Forgot Password” on the login page. If you don’t remember your login information, contact your neighborhood school for assistance in reactivating your ParentVUE account. To begin the enrollment process: Log in using your ParentVUE account. Select the "Enrollment for New Students" icon. This will guide you step by step through the enrollment process. To begin the enrollment process, click on the "Create a New Account" button. Answer the new family questions. Enter your name and email address, you will be sent a welcome email with a link to complete the account creation. Once logged in, select the "Enrollment for New Students" icon. This will guide you step by step through the enrollment process. Everett Public Schools provides full-day, state-funded kindergarten. Families are encouraged to enroll early and attend orientation sessions to help prepare their child and ensure a smooth start to school. Choice programs offer all students, including those with economic or exceptional needs, access to personalized learning that fits their goals. This includes High School Online Options, Sequoia HS, Dual Language, IB and more. Everett Public Schools Early Childhood Education and Assistance Program (ECEAP) is a state-funded, free preschool program for qualifying children and families, designed to support early learning, school readiness, and overall child development. CTE Signature STEM Program offers five industry-led career pathways with certifications, leadership opportunities, scholarships, and free transportation for students attending outside their home high school. Port Gardner School is a parent partnership program that offers engaging onsite classes with certificated teachers and provides resources, guidance, and support for home-based education. Special Services Placement Everett Public Schools offers specialized services for eligible students through a formal referral and evaluation process, ensuring individualized support based on each student’s unique needs. I do not have my documents yet to upload. What do I do? What is the age eligibility for kindergarten students? What is the age eligibility for 1st grade students? Is your high school student planning to participate in athletics? Where can I find the supply list/What supplies do I need? You can continue with online enrollment and submit your application. You can bring your documents to the enrolling school and ask the registrar to update your online enrollment form. You can also email photos of your documents to the registrar email address. See our School Enrollment Contacts page. All children enrolling in kindergarten must be 5 years old by midnight on August 31 of that school year. For more information about early learning programs, go to: Kindergarten webpage All children enrolling in first grade must be 6 years old by midnight on August 31 of that school year. This is the state uniform entry age. If your child is born after August 31 and before November 1 of that school year and has successfully completed a state-approved kindergarten program of 1,000 or more hours, you can enroll your child in first grade following these steps: For a list of approved private schools, visit the Washington State Board of Education website: https://www.sbe.wa.gov/our-work/private-schools. Parent/guardian completes top portion of First Grade Early Entrance Waiver Request form. Your child’s state-approved kindergarten program completes the remainder of the form and sends it with a copy of your child’s final progress report to: Community Resource Center – P-5 Early Learning Programs - Attention Jodi Madison by June 30 Parent/guardian emails proof of age and recent proof of address to Jodi Madison at jmadison2@everettsd.org by June 30. The placement will be provisional until the thirtieth calendar day following the student’s first day of attendance. A final determination of placement will be made at that time. If your high school student is an athlete, check with the athletics director at their new school to verify continued eligibility to participate in athletics. Each schools supply list will be available on the school website under "About Us" > “School Supply List".

Food & Nutrition Services - Everett Public Schools Food & Nutrition Services Everett Public Schools Food & Nutrition Services provides healthy, balanced meals to support student growth and learning in a safe and friendly environment. We are dedicated to building strong bodies and minds through quality meals and individualized service. We offer breakfast and lunch every day at all schools, serving approximately 3,000 breakfasts and 9,000 lunches daily. All meals meet or exceed federal nutrition standards. Our programs include the National School Lunch and Breakfast Programs, After School Snacks, Fresh Fruit and Vegetable Grants, Summer Food Service, and USDA Food Distribution, all guided by USDA regulations. The Child Nutrition Eligibility and Education Benefit (CNEEB) application helps families determine eligibility for free or reduced-price meals and other educational benefits. In addition to free meals, families meeting income guidelines may qualify for additional benefits, including: Increased funding to support student learning Technology support and discounts for internet and some utilities Reduced college application fees Discounted SAT, ACT, and AP test fees, plus scholarship opportunities Healthy, nutritious meals for students Child Nutrition Eligibility and Education Benefit Application Food & Nutrition Services 3900 Broadway, Everett, WA 98201 Explore daily breakfast and lunch menus for each school. See nutritious and balanced meal options planned for students. Menus are updated regularly so families can plan ahead. Meal Prices & Payment Check the cost of school breakfast and lunch meals. Easily add money to your child’s account online or at school. Stay informed and manage payments conveniently. Support student nutrition by contributing to our Food & Nutrition program. Your generous donation helps provide healthy meals for students in need, ensuring they have the energy and focus to succeed in school. Student Wellness Policy Learn about our student wellness policy that promotes healthy eating and nutrition. Guidelines ensure meals and snacks support students’ overall health and well-being. Enjoy nutritious meals even during the summer months. Free or low-cost breakfast and lunch options are available for students. Request a refund for your child’s meal account easily using our form. Quick and simple process to return unused funds. Year end refunds can take up to 8 weeks to process due to the large volume received. Special Dietary Needs Food Safety Guidelines EPS School Event Food and Snack Order Forms Federal law and USDA regulations require nutrition programs to make reasonable modifications to accommodate children with disabilities, which can include allergies and digestive conditions, but does not include personal diet preferences. Requesting Special Meal Accommodations You need to request special meal accommodations if: Your child plans to eat school meals AND Your child has a medically prescribed dietary need that would require us to modify our menu* *Menu note: we do not need accommodation requests for soy milk (we offer this at every school), fish/seafood allergies, treenut or peanut allergies because they do not require menu modification. Vegetarian meals are offered every day. Please include all allergies on your child's annual health history form. To request special meal accommodations, complete the "Request for Special Dietary Accommodations" form at the bottom of this page. This form must be signed by a state-recognized medical provider. Please return the completed form to the Food & Nutrition Department or your school nurse. Food & Nutrition Department Email: foodservices@everettsd.org Please contact us with questions about special meal accommodations at 425-385-4380 Allergen Information Our menu posted on the website can be filtered to identify allergens. In addition, allergens can be viewed along with nutritional information for each food item by selecting the specific item. Only the top 9 allergens are flagged, these allergens are: soy, wheat, milk, peanut, treenut, egg, fish, shellfish and sesame. While there are many more ingredients that can cause allergic reactions, the top 9 are the only ones required on nutrition labels, therefore, these are the ones the menu planner can track. Allergen information is taken directly from manufacturer’s labels and databases and is subject to change without notice. Everett Public School District does not guarantee the information and provides it solely for your convenience. The Food and Nutrition Department does everything we can to accommodate special dietary needs, however, the safest meals are those brought from home. For additional information, please contact the Food and Nutrition Department at 425-385-4380. the Food and Nutrition Department at 425-385-4380. Special Dietary Accommodation Form Request for Special Dietary Accommodations Request for Special Dietary Accommodations - SPANISH Many classroom enrichment activities include food. For the safety of our students and staff, Everett Public Schools Food & Nutrition Department has developed the following food guidelines based on information from Snohomish Health District: Use careful handwashing with soap and hot water before preparing and/or serving food, after using the restroom, after sneezing or coughing, and as necessary when food is being served. Food served to students should be served using disposable gloves or tongs. It is best to use gloves or utensils rather than touch with hands. If gloves or tongs are not available, serve with well-washed hands and use minimal touch. Due to the increase in allergies, please check with the school nurse or teacher for any specific allergies in the classroom. Please read labels carefully: some foods have “hidden” source of allergens (for example, tortilla chips fried in peanut oil). Prepared food from a commercial source is made following strict state and local inspection. For that reason, it is preferred that food items come sealed, from the store. Because of the difficulty of providing adequate refrigeration, foods containing custard or cream fillings are not to be served. Commercial fruit pies are acceptable, but not pumpkin pies or other custard pies made with eggs. Meat dishes, meat-filled pies, sprouts, and potato or macaroni salads should not be served. No home canned products are allowed. Fresh fruit can be used if it is washed and cut immediately before it is to be served. All cut melons are considered potentially hazardous and must be kept ice cold until served. Never serve unpasteurized milk or juice of any kind, especially apple juice or cider. Canned or bottled drinks are preferable to beverages that are mixed and brought in. Disposable cups and utensils are preferable. Minimize handling of all disposable cups, plates, napkins and utensils. Additional Food Safety Tips 1. Things that can make food unsafe to eat include: Viruses, which can get into food by sneezing, coughing and unclean hands. Bacteria, which can grow in food if the food is not kept at proper temperatures. Improper storage and preparation that can cause food to become contaminated. 2. Some foods are more likely than others to grow germs that cause foodborne illnesses. It is very important to keep these foods at proper temperatures to keep germs from growing. Examples of potentially hazardous foods: Milk and dairy products. Meat, poultry, eggs, fish, and shellfish. Cooked potatoes, beans and rice. Cut melons and sprouts. 3. Field Trips: Consider these ideas to keep your child’s field trip lunch safe: Pack only non-perishable foods. Pack a refreezable ice pack or frozen juice box in the lunch. Any perishable foods should be packed in an insulated cooler with refreezable ice packs to insure EPS School Snack Order Form EPS School Event Food Order Form In accordance with federal civil rights law and U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) civil rights regulations and policies, this institution is prohibited from discriminating on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation), disability, age, or reprisal or retaliation for prior civil rights activity. Program information may be made available in languages other than English. Persons with disabilities who require alternative means of communication to obtain program information (e.g., Braille, large print, audiotape, American Sign Language), should contact the responsible state or local agency that administers the program or USDA’s TARGET Center at (202) 720-2600 (voice and TTY) or contact USDA through the Federal Relay Service at (800) 877-8339. To file a program discrimination complaint, a Complainant should complete Form AD-3027, USDA Program Discrimination Complaint Form by clicking the button below from any USDA office, by calling (866) 632-9992, or by writing a letter addressed to USDA. The letter must contain the complainant’s name, address, telephone number, and a written description of the alleged discriminatory action in sufficient detail to inform the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights (ASCR) about the nature and date of an alleged civil rights violation. Form AD-3027, USDA Program Discrimination Complaint Form The completed AD-3027 form or letter must be submitted to USDA by: U.S. Department of Agriculture Office of the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights 1400 Independence Avenue, SW Washington, D.C. 20250-9410; or Fax: (833) 256-1665 or (202) 690-7442; or Email: USDA Program Intake(link is external) This institution is an equal opportunity provider.

Families can conveniently pay for meals, fees, and other school costs online. Secure payment systems accept major credit/debit cards and electronic checks, letting you add funds or make payments anytime without visiting the school office. Payments Online is an internet web service allowing parents or guardians to make payments for Everett Public Schools purchases, fines or fees. Users have student historical receipts available as well as the ability to pay for student library and textbook fines. On a school by school basis there may also be a limited offering of school and district-sponsored items or classes also available for internet purchase. To access specific information about your student, your User Name will be your student's district-assigned student id number and the Password will be that same student's registered last name (this must be entered in UPPERCASE letters). If you have more than one student attending Everett Public Schools, you will log in separately for each one. Currently, there are no convenience fees for purchases made online. This will be reevaluated in future years. If a refund is issued for a payment where a convenience fee has been charged, the convenience fee will not be refunded. Login to Payments Online MySchoolBucks lets families securely pay for student meals, activity fees, and other school-related expenses online or through the mobile app. You can add funds, set up automatic payments, and track balances and purchases anytime. My School Bucks also has a free downloadable app for Android and Apple users. The free My School Bucks mobile app allows users to view balances, make payments, monitor cafeteria purchase history, transfer funds from sibling to sibling, and receive low balance push notifications, all from the palm of their hand. To download the app, search in the Apple or Play Store and search for My School Bucks or MSB Parents. Login to MySchoolBucks You can also pay via cash or checks. Make checks payable to "School Name" or Everett Public Schools. We cannot accept checks without an imprinted name and address. Please write your student's name or ID number in the memo line. Elementary Only: Please provide cash in a sealed and clearly labeled baggie or envelope with your student's First and Last name and student ID number. Everett Public Schools gratefully accepts donations to support student learning and enrichment. Contributions help fund programs, classroom resources, and opportunities that benefit all students. Gifts can be made online.

Call 911 if you are in immediate danger. SafeSchools Alert is not monitored 24 hours a day. Any in-progress or life safety event should be reported immediately to 911. Report concerns about bullying, harassment, drugs, vandalism, or any safety issue quickly and easily with SafeSchools Alert. All reports are reviewed promptly to help maintain a safe and secure learning environment. You can submit a tip in four convenient ways. Every tip submitted to Vector Alert is taken seriously and will be acted upon. Intentionally submitting a tip that you know to be false is a criminal offense. You can now submit tips via the Vector Alert App for Android or Apple smartphones. Choose the corresponding app store below to download the app. To submit tips to your organization, enter the following code in the app when prompted. #1350 Thank you in advance for submitting your tip and doing your part to help make our school community an even safer place to work and learn. Safety at home for students starts with creating routines that help them stay aware and responsible. Simple reminders and open communication help students feel safe and confident at home. Social media safety means thinking before you post and keeping personal information private. Staying aware of who you interact with online helps protect your privacy and well-being. Help is available. You can reach out for help for yourself or to support someone else. If you are in crisis, you can call the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline 24 hours a day, seven days a week, by calling 9-8-8. Everyone needs a little extra support at some time in their life. If you or someone you know needs support now, call or text 988 which connects you with a trained crisis counselor who can help. Support, guidance, and resources are available for students and families dealing with substance abuse, eating disorders, behavioral or mental health challenges to ensure well-being and safety. Report harassment or bullying in a safe and confidential way. This helps the district respond appropriately and maintain a respectful, supportive environment for all.

Transportation - Everett Public Schools The Transportation Department ensures safe, reliable, and efficient bus services for all students. We are committed to providing timely transportation, maintaining well-serviced vehicles, and supporting student safety on every trip to and from school and school-related activities. The district contracts with Durham School Services for school bus drivers and buses. Durham drivers are not district employees, and the buses (with the exception of some special education buses, and vans) are not district property. The Durham contract includes maintenance and repair of school buses, and careful driver safety and training. Transportation Department Services: Schedules and routes daily school bus transportation. Provides buses for vocational education, athletic events, and extracurricular trips. Coordinates with state and local officials on road construction, new housing developments, and conditions affecting student transportation. 2025-26 Find My School & Route 2025-26 Bus Routes by School Kindergarten through 12th-grade students who live more than a half-mile walking distance from school can ride a bus. We may make exceptions for students who live where conditions make walking to and from school hazardous. Eligibility and exceptions are guided by the Washington State Department of Transportation’s School Walk and Bike Routes: A Guide for Planning and Improving Walk and Bike to School Options for Students 3900 Broadway, Everett, WA 98201 Frequently Used Forms Durham School Services Informed Consent Notice: Student Informed Consent Notice: Adult Supervisor Approval For The Use Of Private Vehicle: Employee Approval For The Use Of Private Vehicle: Non-Employee Adult Overnight, Out-of-State, and International Travel Report 2025-26 Permission to Release Student - Authorization for pick-up for non-guardians or 1-12 sped student drop-alone 2025-26 Permission to Release Student-Spanish 2026-27 Permission to Release Student - Authorization for pick-up for non-guardians or 1-12 sped student drop-alone 2026-27 Permission to Release Student - Spanish 2025-26 Request for transportation change - For students wanting to ride a bus to an address other than the primary home address 2025-26 Request for transportation change - Spanish 2026-27 Request for Transportation Change - For students wanting to ride a bus to an address other than the primary home address 2026-27 Request for Transportation Change - Spanish Cedar Wood Elementary Forest View Elementary Hawthorne Elementary Jefferson Elementary Mill Creek Elementary Penny Creek Elementary Silver Firs Elementary Silver Lake Elementary Tambark Creek Elementary View Ridge Elementary The district contracts with Durham School Services for school bus drivers and buses. Durham drivers are not district employees, and the buses (with the exception of some special education buses, cars and vans) are not district property. The Durham contract includes maintenance and repair of school buses, and careful driver safety and training. Durham’s first priority is getting your children to school, safely and on time, ready to learn. We are privileged to serve as the transportation provider for Everett Public Schools. The students are our top priority and we do everything possible to ensure their safety. Customer Service Center 1304 80th St SW, Everett, WA 98203 5:30 AM to 5:30 PM, Monday – Friday Transportation Staff wnoftz@durhamschoolservices.com Operations Supervisor tfinicle@durhamschoolservices.com Safety & Training Supervisor mhardoby@durhamschoolservices.com Getting students to school safely, on time and ready to learn. Following school bus safety tips helps keep students safe on and around the bus. Staying aware, following basic rules, and practicing caution ensures a safer and smoother ride for everyone. Consistently practicing these habits builds good routines that protect both students and drivers. During severe weather, buses may operate on designated snow routes to ensure student safety. These routes are planned to avoid hazardous areas and help maintain reliable transportation when conditions are challenging. Regular Start Time Routes2 Hour Delay Routes The My Stop app lets families track their child’s school bus in real time. It provides estimated arrival times and updates for a safer, more convenient commute. It also helps parents plan their schedules better and stay informed about any delays or route changes.
